# AiXBase

> AI Skills distribution for file-backed database management, SQL, helpdesk ticketing, RDF/OWL ontology generation, and AI-enacted application composition.
> All operations are plain markdown skill files; no runtime, SDK, or build step required.

## Key Resources

- [Skill Manifest](manifest.json) — machine-readable catalog of all skills and bundles
- [Agent Wiki](SKILLS/Agent/Agent.wiki.md) — application composition layer skill reference
- [UniversalSQL Wiki](SKILLS/XBase/UniversalSQL/XBase-UniversalSQL.wiki.md) — SQL translation layer skill reference
- [Ontology Wiki](SKILLS/Ontology/Ontology.wiki.md) — RDF/OWL ontology skill reference
- [XBase Wiki](SKILLS/XBase/XBase.wiki.md) — database engine skill reference
- [Ticketing Wiki](SKILLS/TicketingSystem/TicketingSystem.wiki.md) — ticketing system skill reference
- [Latest Release](https://github.com/StewartScottRogers/AiXBase/releases/latest) — versioned ZIP download

## Install Contract

Download the latest `skills.zip` from Releases. Extract it. Place the markdown files where your AI harness loads skill definitions. No other steps required.

## Bundles

- **Agent** (5 skills, 3 groups) — composition layer; turns a set of skills into a live, never-compiled application enacted by AI emulation, with an external state store, a determinism boundary, and supervision of sub-agent teams. Depends on XBase bundle; composes over any bundle.
- **XBase UniversalSQL** (7 skills, 2 groups) — SQL translation layer; accepts standard SQL, parses to execution plan, dispatches to XBase skills. Includes interactive REPL, explain, and schema DDL extractor. Depends on XBase bundle.
- **Ontology** (13 skills, 8 groups) — generates RDF/OWL from any XBase schema on the fly; includes Admin skills for inspection, comparison, and rebuild. Depends on XBase bundle.
- **XBase** (35 skills, 9 groups) — file-backed database engine using dBASE III binary format. No external database engine required.
- **TicketingSystem** (41 skills, 11 groups) — full helpdesk ticketing system built on XBase. Depends on XBase bundle.

## Harness Compatibility

Any AI agent or runtime that can read a markdown file and follow numbered steps can use these skills. No specific AI platform, operating system, or programming language is required.
